content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA, consisting of NNN **distinct** integers.

Calculate number of pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) (1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N)(1 \le i < j \le N)(1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N), such that 2‚čÖ(Ai‚äēAj)=Ai+Aj2 \cdot (A_i \oplus A_j) = A_i + A_j2‚čÖ(Ai‚Äč‚äēAj‚Äč)=Ai‚Äč+Aj‚Äč, where ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes bitwise XOR.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers NNN ‚ąí-‚ąí the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ąí-‚ąí the elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the number of pairs satisfying constraints.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are 222 pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) satisfying all the conditions.

(i,j)=(1,3)(i,j)= (1, 3)(i,j)=(1,3). Thus, 2‚čÖ(A1‚äēA3)=2‚čÖ(1‚äē3)=2‚čÖ2=42\cdot (A_1\oplus A_3) = 2\cdot (1\oplus 3) = 2\cdot 2 = 42‚čÖ(A1‚Äč‚äēA3‚Äč)=2‚čÖ(1‚äē3)=2‚čÖ2=4. Also, A1+A3=1+3=4A_1+A_3 = 1+3 = 4A1‚Äč+A3‚Äč=1+3=4.
(i,j)=(2,4)(i,j)= (2, 4)(i,j)=(2,4). Thus, 2‚čÖ(A2‚äēA4)=2‚čÖ(2‚äē6)=2‚čÖ4=82\cdot (A_2\oplus A_4) = 2\cdot (2\oplus 6) = 2\cdot 4 = 82‚čÖ(A2‚Äč‚äēA4‚Äč)=2‚čÖ(2‚äē6)=2‚čÖ4=8. Also, A2+A4=2+6=8A_2+A_4 = 2+6 = 8A2‚Äč+A4‚Äč=2+6=8.

(i,j)=(2,5)(i,j) = (2, 5)(i,j)=(2,5). Thus, 2‚čÖ(A2‚äēA5)=2‚čÖ(3‚äē1)=2‚čÖ2=42\cdot (A_2\oplus A_5) = 2\cdot (3\oplus 1) = 2\cdot 2 = 42‚čÖ(A2‚Äč‚äēA5‚Äč)=2‚čÖ(3‚äē1)=2‚čÖ2=4. Also, A2+A5=3+1=4A_2+A_5 = 3+1 = 4A2‚Äč+A5‚Äč=3+1=4.
